message:
fix(parser): release identifier interner on statement boundaries

The StatementParseScratch identifier interner was not being cleared
between parses, so interned identifier Arc<str>s accumulated for the
lifetime of the scratch buffer. Every subsequent parse saw a growing
HashSet — slow hashing, gratuitous Arc<str> retention, and memory
that the retained_bytes() accounting was ignoring entirely.

Changes:

- lexer.rs: add IdentifierInterner::clear() to drop all interned
  identifiers in one call, and retained_bytes() which accounts for
  both the HashSet's capacity-backed slot storage
  (capacity × size_of::<Arc<str>>()) and the byte length of each
  interned value. Add a cfg(test) is_empty() accessor so the parser
  test can assert interner state without exposing internals to
  callers.

- parser.rs: wire StatementParseScratch to clear the interner in two
  places — at the top of reset() alongside the tokens/errors reset,
  and at the end of parse_statements_with_scratch_inner() once the
  parser has produced its Statements (the Arc<str>s that need to live
  on have already been cloned into the AST by that point). Fold
  identifier_interner.retained_bytes() into the existing
  retained_bytes() accounting so memory telemetry stops understating
  scratch size.

Regression test (test_parse_statements_with_scratch_releases_identifier_interns):
parse a 32-distinct-identifier SELECT, assert the interner is empty
immediately after the parse returns, then call reset() and assert it
remains empty. Catches any future regression where a parse path
forgets to hand the interner back to the statement-scoped pool.
